standard_services:
The Service provides the end-to-end management of the ‘SAS’ application (The Application) from an functional applicative perspective. Together with the underlaying obligatory Platform services, Cegeka unburdens the customer with all functional and technical aspects from infrastructure up to the functional Application.
The Standard Service features consist out of:

pricing:
Changes are billed on a monthly basis.
Changes as result of Pre-approved and Normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact.
Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.

| Service | Application Management for ‘SAS’ - Argenta | | Unique ID | PRD.A.L.002 | | Service Model | Application Management Service (AMS). This service is dependant of other layers (IaaS and/or PaaS) which are indicated in the ‘Obligatory’ chapter of this document and which have their own Service Description. The combination of the layers assures the customer of the ‘infrastructure up to the functional application’ management. These obligatorily services are called the ‘Platform’. |
Service Application Management for ‘SAS’ - Argenta Unique ID PRD.A.L.002 Service Model Application Management Service (AMS). This service is dependant of other layers (IaaS and/or PaaS) which are indicated in the ‘Obligatory’ chapter of this document and which have their own Service Description. The combination of the layers assures the customer of the ‘infrastructure up to the functional application’ management. These obligatorily services are called the ‘Platform’.
The ‘SAS’ application.

This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features.
The following optional features are possible with the Service:
The application knowledge resides at Cegeka’s partners ‘SAS institute’ and ‘OAK’.

Application Management
Functional Maintenance (by OAK) entails all non-infra and non-technical SAS Solution related issues and new requests.
Typical activities as part of functional maintenance are:
Technical Application Management
Technical Application Management (by SAS) includes the following tasks:
Single point of contact
Cegeka will act as Single Point Of Contact. The following drawing summarizes the responsibilities:
In case of questions during an incident or request, OAK or SAS will contact the Argenta subject matter expert directly, outside these flows. SAS will inform Cegeka of the status of all tickets, so Cegeka can perform the E2E follow-up.
Monitoring events will also be logged by the Cegeka Service desk and dispatched to SAS if applicable.
Tactical Application Management meeting
Argenta, Cegeka, OAK and SAS hold each quarter a tactical meeting.
Examples of topics to discuss include (but are not limited to):
The meeting agenda will be defined up front based on questions and requirements of the three parties at that time.
Operational Application Management meeting
Cegeka, OAK and SAS hold an operational meeting, where the following topics are discussed:

Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Application. Automation is applied where possible.
Proactive management
Housekeeping includes following regular checks:
Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA.
Patching
The service includes patch releases.
Additional patching can be done on RFC basis.
Patching of the underlying platform is part of the Service of that platform.
Life Cycle Management
Cegeka monitors the Technology version of the service and notifies the Customer in accordance with the agreed LCM policy (see cross-functional Services) when used versions are subject to end-of-support or end-of-life. Appropriate actions (updates, replacement or other actions) are planned in accordance with agreed maintenance windows and will be executed on T&M basis in case of major updates. Patches and minor updates are included in the service cost as part of ‘Patching’.

Cegeka monitors the correct working of the Application.
In order to manage the environment properly, application monitoring is set up. This is done in Cegeka's monitoring platform, so that the monitoring of the SAS platform is treated in the same way as the rest of the Argenta landscape.
Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service and are based on evaluation of incidents, problem management or other suggestions.
The underlaying platform is also monitored according to the related Service Description of the platform.
Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management.

Management of users
User & Access Management for SAS accounts is included in this Service.
Detection of security incidents
The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies.
Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders.
SIEM
Additional monitoring can be done based on System and/or Application logs, which can be forwarded to a Customer SIEM solution (Security reason) or other solution (compliance reason, e.g. central collection of application logs to oversee the application stack) on request.
Underlaying Platform hardening
The underlaying platform and OS will be hardened as part of and according to the Service Description of the related Services.

The operational Backup Plan contains the information on frequency and retention of the backups in accordance to the Customer backup policy.
Restores can be done both on file and on full system level. These can be requested via the regular RFC process.
Duration of a restore is dependant of the request (file or full; size of the item(s) to be restored).
Application settings are subject to the backup of the underlaying platform.

Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals).
Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals).
The customer is responsible for the installation of the software during the release, based in the provided binaries and manual.

Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). | request type | Description | Lead time | | Service Requests | (no specific Service Requests for this Service) | | | | | | | Normal Changes | Any other request. Examples: Life Cycle upgrades of the application | Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request. |
request type Description Lead time Service Requests (no specific Service Requests for this Service) Normal Changes Any other request. Examples: Life Cycle upgrades of the application Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request.

This Service is delivered remotely by the sub-outsourcer.

The Cross-functional reporting is part of this Service Reporting where it is applicable for this service (see section ‘Relation to Cross-Functional Services’).
There is no specific (recurrent) reporting for this Service.

Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service.
Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service.
In case of assistance in investigations of functional issues, Cegeka personnel may see or receive fragments of such data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of exports, screen sharing, screen shots, etc. for investigation purposes).

This service is delivered with:
Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues):
(none)
Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party):
OAK
SAS
n/a

The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: | Cross-Functional Service | Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services | | Incident management | In period of closures (month, quarter or year), the Customer can indicate that incidents must receive a higher priority. | | Anti-Malware management | This service is not subject to Anti-Malware management. Anti-Malware management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. | | Vulnerability management & compliancy management (hardening) | This service is not subject to Anti-Malware management. Vulnerability & compliancy management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. |
Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services Incident management In period of closures (month, quarter or year), the Customer can indicate that incidents must receive a higher priority. Anti-Malware management This service is not subject to Anti-Malware management. Anti-Malware management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. Vulnerability management & compliancy management (hardening) This service is not subject to Anti-Malware management. Vulnerability & compliancy management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. All Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.

Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.
Availability
The availability of this Service depends on the availability of the underlying platform and the correct functioning of the operating system.

The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.
The Service Window for this service is ‘Bronze’.
